machinery_genes.role: bitopic inner-membrane platform component (links to secretin)
machinery_genes.quote: XcpQ has been proposed to interact with bitopic inner membrane protein XcpP (GspC)
machinery_genes.note: de Groot et al 2001, P. aeruginosa Xcp system. XcpP = GspC, the inner-membrane component that contacts the secretin. xcpP is the first gene of the xcpPQ operon named in the sys_instance_label.

machinery_genes.quote: The only outer membrane component of the secreton is XcpQ (GspD)
machinery_genes.note: de Groot et al 2001. XcpQ = GspD, the sole outer-membrane protein; forms a ring-shaped multimer (confirmed also in Bleves et al 1999, PMID 10383969).

machinery_genes.quote: On the basis of sequence analyses, XcpRE is the only protein of the P. aeruginosa GSP which does not have a hydrophobic domain. All GspE proteins have sequences similar to Walker A and Walker B boxes.
machinery_genes.note: Filloux, Michel & Bally 1998 FEMS review of the P. aeruginosa Xcp system. Combined notation 'XcpRE' = XcpR/GspE; the cytoplasmic ATP-binding (Walker A/B) ATPase. Also named XcpR (GspE) in Arts et al 2007 (PMID 17464073).

machinery_genes.quote: One of the core components of the Xcp system is the inner-membrane protein XcpS (GspF)
machinery_genes.note: Arts et al 2007, P. aeruginosa. XcpS = GspF, stabilized by co-expression of XcpR (GspE) and XcpY (GspL). FEMS review (PMID 9818381) adds 'XcpSF is predicted to be a polytopic integral cytoplasmic membrane protein.'

machinery_genes.quote: We used XcpT, the major pseudopilin from the type II secretion machinery of Pseudomonas aeruginosa, as a model to study this process.
machinery_genes.note: Arts et al 2006, P. aeruginosa. XcpT is the major pseudopilin; Xcp-Gsp pairing XcpTG (= GspG) given in the FEMS review (PMID 9818381): 'The proteins XcpTG, -UH, -VI, and -WJ are synthesized as precursors with a short, positively charged leader peptide...'

machinery_genes.quote: The proteins XcpTG, -UH, -VI, and -WJ are synthesized as precursors with a short, positively charged leader peptide followed by a highly hydrophobic domain.
machinery_genes.note: Filloux et al 1998 FEMS review. Combined notation '-UH' = XcpU/GspH, one of the four type-IV-pilin-like pseudopilins of the P. aeruginosa Xcp machinery (also Bleves et al 1998, PMID 9466253: 'XcpT, U, V and W ... were therefore designated pseudopilins').

machinery_genes.quote: The proteins XcpTG, -UH, -VI, and -WJ are synthesized as precursors with a short, positively charged leader peptide followed by a highly hydrophobic domain.
machinery_genes.note: Filloux et al 1998 FEMS review. Combined notation '-VI' = XcpV/GspI. One of the four pseudopilins named in Bleves et al 1998 (PMID 9466253).

machinery_genes.quote: The proteins XcpTG, -UH, -VI, and -WJ are synthesized as precursors with a short, positively charged leader peptide followed by a highly hydrophobic domain.
machinery_genes.note: Filloux et al 1998 FEMS review. Combined notation '-WJ' = XcpW/GspJ. One of the four pseudopilins named in Bleves et al 1998 (PMID 9466253).

machinery_genes.quote: We, therefore, classified XcpX (GspK) as the fifth pseudopilin of the system.
machinery_genes.note: Bleves et al 1998, P. aeruginosa. XcpX = GspK, a bitopic cytoplasmic-membrane pseudopilin processed by PilD/XcpA. FEMS review (PMID 9818381) corroborates: 'XcpXK ... also belongs to the pseudopilin family.'

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (binds ATPase XcpR; cytoplasmic N-terminal domain)
machinery_genes.quote: XcpS was shown to be stabilized by co-expression of the XcpR (GspE) and XcpY (GspL) components of the machinery, demonstrating an interaction between these three proteins.
machinery_genes.note: Arts et al 2007, P. aeruginosa. XcpY = GspL. FEMS review (PMID 9818381) adds 'XcpYL presents a large N-terminal domain in the cytoplasm.'

machinery_genes.quote: it was shown that XcpPC and XcpZM are bitopic inner membrane proteins with a large C-terminal domain protruding into the periplasm
machinery_genes.note: Filloux et al 1998 FEMS review. Combined notation 'XcpZM' = XcpZ/GspM, the terminal gene of the xcpR-Z (xcpPQRSTUVWXYZ) operon. de Groot et al 2001 (PMID 11208795) names the full operon: 'xcpPQRSTUVWXYZ'.

machinery_genes.role: prepilin peptidase (matures the pseudopilins; identical to PilD, shared with type-IV pilus system)
machinery_genes.quote: We show that XcpX could be processed by the prepilin peptidase, PilD/XcpA
machinery_genes.note: XcpA = GspO = PilD, the prepilin peptidase that matures the Xcp pseudopilins (XcpT-X). Encoded outside the xcpP-Z operon (de Groot et al 2001, PMID 11208795: 'requires at least 12 xcp gene products (XcpA and XcpP to -Z)'; xcpA at a separate chromosomal locus). FEMS review (PMID 9818381): 'XcpAO was shown to be identical to PilD.'

notes: The Xcp system is the archetypal Pseudomonas aeruginosa T2SS (the 'main terminal branch' of the general secretory pathway), characterized over decades by the Filloux/Tommassen/Lazdunski groups in P. aeruginosa itself (PAO1 / PAK background) - so no cross-strain transfer was needed. The apparatus consists of 12 components named XcpA and XcpP-Z (de Groot et al 2001: 'requires at least 12 xcp gene products (XcpA and XcpP to -Z)'). The xcpP-Z genes are arranged in two divergent operons (xcpPQ and xcpR-Z = xcpPQRSTUVWXYZ); xcpA is at a separate chromosomal locus and is identical to pilD, the type-IV-pilus prepilin peptidase. All 12 components are documented gene-by-gene with verbatim naming quotes, and each Xcp letter is mapped to its unified Gsp family using the combined 'XcpRE / XcpSF / XcpTG ...' notation of the 1998 FEMS review: XcpP=GspC, XcpQ=GspD, XcpR=GspE, XcpS=GspF, XcpT=GspG, XcpU=GspH, XcpV=GspI, XcpW=GspJ, XcpX=GspK, XcpY=GspL, XcpZ=GspM, XcpA=GspO/PilD. Roles for the minor pseudopilins XcpU/V/W draw on the pseudopilin-family quote (Filloux 1998; Bleves 1998 names XcpT-W as pseudopilins) plus the Gsp brief vocabulary. Deliberately excluded as non-machinery: the secreted exoproteins/effectors (elastase LasB, lipase, exotoxin A, phospholipases, alkaline phosphatase - listed in Akrim et al 1993), the upstream Sec/Tat inner-membrane translocon (general export, not the T2SS apparatus), and transcriptional regulators (the divergent xcp operons share growth-phase-dependent regulation per Akrim et al 1993, but the regulators themselves are not apparatus). No GspN/GspS-type accessory genes are part of the Xcp operon (it runs P-Z). XcpA/PilD is included because, although shared with the T4 pilus system, the literature explicitly ties it to maturation of the Xcp pseudopilins. All quotes are verbatim from the cited PMC/journal full texts or PubMed abstracts; all DOIs and PMIDs verified via get_article_metadata.
